glossary.definition: The Voter ID card issued by the Election Commission of India.
glossary.extra: Issued after successful registration on electoral roll.

glossary.definition: A device used for casting votes in elections.
A device that provides a paper trail of votes cast.
Guidelines issued by ECI for parties and candidates during elections.

questions.question: What does EVM stand for?
questions.explanation: EVM stands for Electronic Voting Machine, used in Indian elections since 1982.

questions.question: What is the minimum voting age in India?
questions.explanation: The minimum voting age in India is 18 years.

questions.question: Which body conducts Lok Sabha elections?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India (ECI) conducts Lok Sabha elections.

checklist.title: Voter ID Card
checklist.description: Ensure you have your EPIC (Voter ID) ready.

checklist.title: Check Electoral Roll
checklist.description: Verify your name is on the electoral roll.

general.title: Election Announcement
general.description: The Election Commission announces the election schedule.
details: Model Code of Conduct comes into effect

general.title: Election Campaign
general.description: Parties and candidates campaign for votes.

general.title: Polling Day
general.description: Citizens cast their votes at polling booths.

general.title: Counting of Votes
general.description: Votes are counted and results declared.

state.title: State Election Announcement
state.description: The ECI announces state assembly election schedule.

state.title: Nomination Filing
state.description: Candidates file nominations for assembly seats.

state.title: State Polling Day
state.description: Voters cast ballots for state assembly.

local.title: Local Body Election Announcement
local.description: State Election Commission announces local body elections.
